_comment: OVERNIGHT break-finding sweep on the game's default map (the planet). One neutral 'probe' strategy so every axis is a clean parameter (games == rule-sets). Axes push the key cost/economy knobs toward their extremes to find where the game breaks (runaway / collapse / non-convergence) or goes degenerate (single-city dominance, roads never built, network underfunded). 8 axes -> 5*3*3*4*4*3*3*3 = 19440 games. At ~1-2 games/s on ~20 workers this is a few hours; it is crash-safe (append-on-batch + manifest checkpoint every 100 games) and Ctrl-C-safe (partial jsonl + partial:true manifest kept). Resize the axes to taste. Run: node harness.js sweep_planet_overnight.json --jobs <cpus-1>  (or npm run sweep:overnight). Then: npm run analyze:planet
